Exhibitionist Priest/01 - Zettelkastem/D.filePath: Exhibitionist Priest/01 - Zettelkastem/Drafts/English/02 - Lucas’ Call English - First draft.md
footnoteContents: M: Should we do more like... **He laughs** type thing or do you prefer typing it out? okay formatting text doesnt work in these things, thats meant to be italicized.

S: Hmm the thing is as it's destined to be a visual novel, every action verbs used to describe how someone talks can break the flow like the *He laughs* will have to be put into a different dialogue bubble than "Oh I'm just teasing you" and i think it's easier to put it inside the dialogue directly than to create another bubble solely to describe an action. I sometimes break this rule for a meaningful action but when an onomatopoeia can be used instead, i don't bother

M: My biggest concern is that it sort of breaks the established flow within the text itself by swapping betwen onomatopoeia, partially because within english writing rules this is one of those things where we are taught young to "pick one" and be consistent with, as far as the flow of creative writing. We can just roll with it how it is, but as for a localization thing, it's something to consider/put a pin in for now and see how it feels in implementation. I was imagining the text bubble for these scene being "Oh, I'm just teasing you." _He laughs_ as one continuous thought and not two separate bubbles.
Yeah but on the other hand, i think it breaks the flow to make a new bubble just to say he's laughing
You misunderstand what I'm saying, it wouldn't be two bubbles but 1!
I don't see how that would work in a vn environment

footnoteContents: M: Did you mean excited? Euphoric is a bit more physical in english contexts, even over the top of ecstasy, which is why it was used in the contexts of something like the show Euphoria in reference to drug use. Totally okay if you meant it this way, just needed to give context for the english understanding of the word
S: ok we'll go for ecstasy then

Exhibitionist Priest/01 - Zettelkastem/D.text: It’s as if I were walking through a Styrofoam castle. ~~Numb~~Dead to the touch and devoid of any warmth
Exhibitionist Priest/01 - Zettelkastem/D.filePath: Exhibitionist Priest/01 - Zettelkastem/Drafts/English/01 - Grandma English - Murkwater first draft.md
footnoteContents: M: Leaving a note here to see the original intent of the tactile element here. I wouldnt say styrofoam normally evokes a numb feeling in my mind!

S: yeah maybe it's a translation issue or just my weird sensory system but having numb fingers for me is like not feeling anything but the nothing is replaced cold (as devoid of warmth) tingling sensation who is very bothering to me. Styrofoam is also like that for me : when you rub it, you get this unpleasing tingling sensation coupled with the absence of warmth of the material
M: In a weird way "fuzzy" can evoke what you're talking about here, making the edit now.

S: i don't really now about that, i wanted to convey a sense of apathy as well and fuzzy doesn't convey that at least for me. That's also what the styrofoam castle metaphor is for: a numbing all white environment devoid of any colors

M: Fuzzy in english is a complicated word. When translated over to other languages it often denotes something is soft, or has fur, but contextually it can represent a lack of feeling. Like pins and needles, if you're familiar with that term. Numb to the touch is more like...touching fiberglass. It's hard to describe but we can explore more terms overall that can better help express the sensation because I understand now what you're going for.
S: yay do you have other words instead of fuzzy? if we don't find any we'll just put fuzzy)
M: I will do someresearch for you! We'll tap back on this
S: ok!
M: Via DMs, discussed dead to the touch, the preferred option
